“Halt. State your business.” One of them stopped me and asked me about my business.

“Lady Zara has asked for my presence,” I replied without much thought.

“Wait here for now. Lady Catherine is currently discussing some important matters with Lady Zara.” The knightess replied without much emotion. She didn’t see me beneath her or anything. She was just doing her job.

“Understood. But if you could, please relay my presence to her. I don’t want to be punished for being late.” I replied politely.

She looked at her companion and then nodded. She then knocked on the door.

“Who is it?” Zara’s voice came from the inside.

“Lady Zara. You have a visitor.” The knight informed her.

“Who is it?” Zara asked.

The knight glanced at me, and I quickly replied, “Jack.”

“It is Jack, my lady.” The knight replied.

“Let him in,” Zara said.

The knightess hesitated, but she didn’t impede me from entering.

I opened the door and entered, and to my surprise, I saw who Catherine was.

“Why have you allowed this filthy commoner to enter?” Carlson’s daughter-in-law asked with a scowl on her face.

And Zara, without caring for her opinion, called me closer to her.

What the…?
